title: Telangana heatwave intensifies; Khammam sizzles at 45°C - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: Heatwave conditions across Telangana continued with Khammam, in the last 24-hours recording the State's highest at 45 degree Celsius, India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad weather data on Thursday said.
Meanwhile, Hakimpet recorded the highest in Hyderabad at 41.3 degrees Celsius.
According to IMD data, a heatwave has formed over northern and eastern districts of Telangana. After Khammam, the next district to record the highest temperature (in degree Celsius) was Ramagundam (44.6), Nizamabad (44.4), and Adilabad at 44.3.
The northern districts, including Adilabad and Nizamabad, experienced severe warm night conditions, with minimum temperatures at 32.7 and 32.3, respectively.
According to IMD weather data, Hyderabad recorded a maximum of 39.5 i.e., Begumpet station, while other weather monitoring stations at Hakimpet recorded 41.3, and Dundigal recorded 41 degrees Celsius.
Top 5 in TS (IMD, data): In degrees Celsius
Khammam: 45
Ramagundam: 44.6
Nizamabad: 44.4

Bhadrachalam: 43.8
